examples.title: Electrolyzer Process for Hydrogen Production
examples.description: Basic algorithm for water electrolysis process
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Electrolysis(inputEnergy, waterVolume)
BEGIN
 SET hydrogenOutput = 0
 SET oxygenOutput = 0
 
 WHILE waterVolume > 0 DO
 // Apply electrical energy to split water molecules
 APPLY voltage TO electrodes IN electrolyzer
 
 // At anode: 2H2O → O2 + 4H+ + 4e-
 RELEASE oxygen AT anode
 
 // At cathode: 2H+ + 2e- → H2
 RELEASE hydrogen AT cathode
 
 hydrogenOutput = hydrogenOutput + (waterVolume * H_PER_WATER)
 oxygenOutput = oxygenOutput + (waterVolume * O_PER_WATER)
 waterVolume = waterVolume - 1
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN (hydrogenOutput, oxygenOutput)
END

// Efficiency calculation
FUNCTION CalculateEfficiency(electricalInput, hydrogenOutput)
BEGIN
 RETURN (hydrogenEnergy / electricalInput) * 100
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode represents the basic electrolysis process where water is split into hydrogen and oxygen using electrical energy. The efficiency typically ranges from 70-85% depending on technology.

examples.title: Fuel Cell Power Generation
examples.description: Algorithm representing electricity generation in a hydrogen fuel cell
examples.code:
PROCEDURE FuelCellPower(hydrogenInput, oxygenInput)
BEGIN
 SET electricalEnergy = 0
 SET waterOutput = 0
 SET heatOutput = 0
 
 WHILE hydrogenInput > 0 AND oxygenInput > 0 DO
 // Hydrogen oxidation at anode
 hydrogenAtAnode = SPLIT(hydrogenInput, catalyst)
 protons = hydrogenAtAnode.protons
 electrons = hydrogenAtAnode.electrons
 
 // Electrons flow through external circuit (electricity)
 electricalEnergy = electrons.current * voltage
 
 // Protons pass through membrane
 protonsThroughMembrane = PASS(protons, membrane)
 
 // Oxygen reduction at cathode
 waterProduced = COMBINE(protonsThroughMembrane, oxygenInput, electrons)
 waterOutput = waterOutput + waterProduced
 
 // Heat generated as byproduct
 heatOutput = CalculateHeat(electricalEnergy)
 
 hydrogenInput = hydrogenInput - 1
 oxygenInput = oxygenInput - 1
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN (electricalEnergy, waterOutput, heatOutput)
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates how fuel cells generate electricity by combining hydrogen and oxygen through an electrochemical reaction, producing water and heat as byproducts.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) - depends on hydrogen flow rate

examples.title: Steam Methane Reforming Process
examples.description: Industrial hydrogen production via steam methane reforming
examples.explanation: SMR is the dominant hydrogen production method. It involves two main reactions: reforming (endothermic) and water gas shift (exothermic), followed by CO2 capture for blue hydrogen.
